distance_km.note: Search results show the 40th Viru Maraton (January 17, 2026) had distances of 35km and 18km due to weather shortening. However, the standard/planned distances are 42km and 21km according to multiple sources. The claim of 42km matches the planned full distance, but recent races have been shortened. Most recent confirmed result shows 36km as the actual race distance on 17-01-2026.

elevation_m.note: No web sources in search results provide elevation gain data for Viru Maraton. Cannot confirm or deny the 250m claim.

country.note: Multiple sources confirm Estonia as the country. Located in Mõedaku, Lääne-Viru County (Virumaa).

region.note: Confirmed as Virumaa (Lääne-Viru County) in multiple sources.

discipline.note: Search results indicate 'Free Technique' (FT) not 'classic'. The Worldloppet results table shows Viru Maraton races as 'FT' (Free Technique), which allows both classic and skate techniques.

typical_participants.note: No web sources provide typical participant numbers. Cannot confirm or deny the 450 claim.

year_founded.note: Search results indicate the 2023 edition was the 37th race and the 2026 edition was the 40th race, but do not provide the founding year. This would suggest founding around 1987-1989, but this is inference rather than explicit source confirmation.

official_website.note: Search results provide the official website as www.estoloppet.ee (Viru Maraton is part of the Estoloppet series).

typical_race_date.note: Multiple sources confirm the race typically occurs in January, not February. The 37th edition was January 28, 2023, and the 40th edition was January 17, 2026. It is the season opening event of Estoloppet.
